raw_excerpt_key_facts: Verbatim from the live federalregister.gov page (fetched 2026-06-05 ~10:25 CT for cross-check): [Document Details] Document Number 2026-07361; Document Citation 91 FR 20465; Pages 20465-20467; Publication Date 04/16/2026; Document Type Notice; Agencies: Department of Health and Human Services, Food and Drug Administration. [Agency/Docket Number] Docket No. FDA-2025-N-6895. [Dates Text verbatim] 'The meeting will be held on July 23, 2026, from 8:00 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. Eastern Time and July 24, 2026, from 8:00 a.m. to 3:50 p.m. Eastern Time.' [Public Inspection] 'This PDF is FR Doc. 2026-07361 as it appeared on Public Inspection on 04/15/2026 at 8:45 am.' [Filing stamp] 'FR Doc. 2026-07361 Filed 4-15-26; 8:45 am]. [BILLING] BILLING CODE 4164-01-P. [Signature] 'Grace R. Graham, Deputy Commissioner for Policy, Legislation, and International Affairs.' [Agenda verbatim] 'On July 23, 2026, the Committee will discuss the following bulk drug substances being considered for inclusion on the 503A Bulks List: BPC-157-related bulk drug substances (BPC-157 (free base)/BPC-157 acetate), KPV-related bulk drug substances (KPV (free base)/KPV acetate), TB-500-related bulk drug substances (TB-500 (free base)/TB-500 acetate), and MOTs-C-related bulk drug substances (MOTs-C (free base)/MOTs-C acetate).' [Table] BPC-157/BPC-157 acetate: Ulcerative colitis (UC); KPV/KPV acetate: Wound healing and inflammatory conditions; TB-500/TB-500 acetate: Wound healing; MOTs-C/MOTs-C acetate: Obesity and osteoporosis. [July 24 verbatim] 'On July 24, 2026, the Committee will discuss the following bulk drug substances being considered for inclusion on the 503A Bulks List: Emideltide (also referred to as delta sleeping inducing peptide (DSIP))-related bulk drug substances (Emideltide (free base)/Emideltide acetate), Semax-related bulk drug substances (Semax (free base)/Semax acetate), and Epitalon-related bulk drug substances (Epitalon (free base)/Epitalon acetate).' [Table] Emideltide/acetate: Opioid withdrawal, chronic insomnia, and narcolepsy; Semax/acetate: Cerebral ischemia, migraine, and trigeminal neuralgia; Epitalon/acetate: Insomnia. [Public-hearing sessions verbatim] 'The sessions will begin at approximately 10:15 a.m., 11:50 a.m., 2:15 p.m., and 4:00 p.m. on July 23, 2026 Eastern Time. The sessions will begin at approximately 10:20 a.m., 12:30 p.m., and 3:20 p.m. on July 24, 2026 Eastern Time.' [Oral presentation request deadline] 'on or before June 30, 2026'. [Docket close] 'The docket will close on July 22, 2026.' [Comments to Committee] 'Comments received on or before July 9, 2026, will be provided to the Committee.' [Contact verbatim] 'Takyiah Stevenson, Center for Drug Evaluation and Research, Food and Drug Administration, 10903 New Hampshire Ave., Bldg. 31, Rm. 2417, Silver Spring, MD 20993-0002, 240-402-2507, email: PCAC@fda.hhs.gov'. [ADDRESSES verbatim] 'FDA White Oak Campus, 10903 New Hampshire Ave., Bldg. 31 Conference Center, the Great Room (Rm. 1503), Silver Spring, MD 20993-0002.' [Document Statistics] 'Page views 1,309 as of 06/05/2026 at 10:15 am EDT'. [Regulations.gov Data] Comment Due Date 07/22/2026; Docket ID FDA-2025-N-6895; 65 comments.
diff_against_researcher_capture: No substantive differences. Researcher captured the same headline facts (docket, document number, dates, location, agenda, contact, signatures, signed-by line). Verifier independently confirmed all headline facts from the live federalregister.gov page.
